CoreFinding: Runtime identity is allowed; persisted real identity or environment data is forbidden
FalsePositiveGuard: Automated matches require contextual review and public attribution is preserved
AcceptanceEvidence: Synthetic artifact passes; marked real artifact blocks; runtime SELECT contract remains

PermissionScope: VIEW SERVER STATE and msdb/HADR dependent
CoreFinding: Corruption indicators, verification gaps and repair evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: No indicator is presented as proof of integrity; DBCC is not auto-executed
AcceptanceEvidence: Controlled page/check evidence plus empty and permission-denied cases

PermissionScope: VIEW SERVER STATE/PERFORMANCE STATE and database metadata
CoreFinding: File, volume, autogrowth and exhaustion risk
FalsePositiveGuard: No time-to-full without sufficient history; file free and volume free remain separate
AcceptanceEvidence: Fixed/percent growth, maxsize, low-volume and no-history cases

PermissionScope: VIEW SERVER STATE/PERFORMANCE STATE and XE access
CoreFinding: Non-yielding, memory, dump, severe and health-component evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: Existing sessions are read only; one-shot diagnostics; event absence is not health proof
AcceptanceEvidence: Controlled event parsing, no-target, denied and bounded-time cases

CoreFinding: Typed snapshot, rate, delta and fraction counters
FalsePositiveGuard: cntr_type, base, sample interval and reset epoch are mandatory
AcceptanceEvidence: Snapshot/delta/fraction/reset fixtures calculate correct units

PrimarySources: Query Store catalog|plan feedback|query variants|automatic tuning
PermissionScope: Database state and Query Store permissions
CoreFinding: PSP, OPPO, feedback, forcing and tuning state
FalsePositiveGuard: Variants and feedback are not automatically classified as defects
AcceptanceEvidence: 2019 unsupported, 2022 PSP and 2025 OPPO capability cases

PermissionScope: VIEW SERVER STATE/PERFORMANCE STATE and database access
CoreFinding: Latch, spinlock and hot-page deltas
FalsePositiveGuard: Two samples and reset checks; no causal claim from spinlock name alone
AcceptanceEvidence: Delta/reset/page-resolution/permission cases

CoreFinding: Buffer distribution and internal/external pressure evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: No max-memory recommendation from a single snapshot
AcceptanceEvidence: Bounded scan, empty cache, pressure and denied cases

CoreFinding: LSN chain, forks, checksums, damage and restore evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: msdb history and VERIFYONLY never prove recoverability
AcceptanceEvidence: Full/diff/log, copy-only, fork, pruned history and AG cases

CoreFinding: Untrusted constraints, index design and exhaustion risks
FalsePositiveGuard: No automatic DDL recommendation without workload and semantic context
AcceptanceEvidence: Controlled trusted/untrusted, overlap and exhaustion cases

CoreFinding: Skew, tail, modification and partition variation evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: Skew is an indicator not proof of a bad plan or out-of-range value; candidates are prelimited
AcceptanceEvidence: Uniform/skewed/filtered/incremental/bounded/denied cases

PermissionScope: HADR and VIEW SERVER STATE/PERFORMANCE STATE
CoreFinding: Repair, seeding, quorum, lease, routing and distributed-AG state
FalsePositiveGuard: Catch-up time from a snapshot remains an estimate
AcceptanceEvidence: No-AG, primary, secondary, suspended, seeding and denied cases

CoreFinding: Missing critical alerts, schedule collisions and notification health
FalsePositiveGuard: Configuration absence is contextual; recipient data is runtime only
AcceptanceEvidence: No-Agent, missing-alert, colliding-job, mail-failure and denied cases

CoreFinding: Evidence-backed severity and confidence
FalsePositiveGuard: Observation, confidence, counter-evidence and data gaps remain separate
AcceptanceEvidence: Multi-signal, contradictory-signal and missing-source cases

PrimarySources: XTP table memory|hash index|memory consumers|checkpoint files|transactions
CoreFinding: Memory, hash-chain, checkpoint and transaction pressure
FalsePositiveGuard: Module runs only when XTP is present
AcceptanceEvidence: No-XTP, hash-skew, memory-pressure and checkpoint cases

CoreFinding: History growth, retention and structural consistency
FalsePositiveGuard: Module runs only when temporal objects exist
AcceptanceEvidence: No-temporal, growing-history, retention and disabled-versioning cases

CoreFinding: Backlog, disabled queue, activation and conversation growth
FalsePositiveGuard: Message body is never selected; payload is unnecessary
AcceptanceEvidence: No-Broker, aged-backlog, disabled-queue and poison cases

CoreFinding: Population, crawl, backlog and memory health
FalsePositiveGuard: Module runs only when full-text objects exist
AcceptanceEvidence: No-fulltext, active, stalled and denied cases

CoreFinding: Cleanup, retention, capture and distribution lag
FalsePositiveGuard: Remote/topology gaps are reported, not treated as healthy
AcceptanceEvidence: No-feature, cleanup lag, invalid-version and remote-denied cases

CoreFinding: TDE and certificate dependency state
FalsePositiveGuard: Secrets, private keys and passwords are never selected
AcceptanceEvidence: No-TDE, encrypting, encrypted, dependency-gap and denied cases

CoreFinding: Select applicable deep modules from actual feature use
FalsePositiveGuard: Inventory is not a health verdict
AcceptanceEvidence: Feature-absent and one-case-per-feature capability tests

CoreFinding: Paused, abandoned, overlapping or resource-heavy maintenance
FalsePositiveGuard: Long duration alone is not classified without progress and schedule context
AcceptanceEvidence: Paused/resumed/rollback/overlap cases

PrimarySources: Selected monitor outputs with reset epochs
CoreFinding: Trends, deltas, anomalies and restart-safe baselines
FalsePositiveGuard: Requires retention, size, privacy and deletion design first
AcceptanceEvidence: Restart, retention, purge, UTC, partition and privacy acceptance suite

CoreFinding: Version, configuration and performance drift across instances
FalsePositiveGuard: Tenant and environment isolation required
AcceptanceEvidence: Multi-instance isolation, failure and retention tests

CoreFinding: End-to-end recoverability and host/storage evidence
FalsePositiveGuard: T-SQL indicators are never presented as external proof
AcceptanceEvidence: Isolated restore plus CHECKDB and platform-specific runbook evidence
